text.title: MVP Scoping Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding MVP Scoping. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Riskiest assumption named in one sentence before scoping.
rule.rationale: Riskiest assumption named in one sentence before scoping.

rule.statement: Minimum end-to-end journey is the scope baseline.
rule.rationale: Minimum end-to-end journey is the scope baseline.

rule.statement: Now/Not-Now policy defends scope under pressure.
rule.rationale: Now/Not-Now policy defends scope under pressure.

rule.statement: Build cap ≤2 weeks; longer = re-scope or pivot to Micro-MVP series.
rule.rationale: Build cap ≤2 weeks; longer = re-scope or pivot to Micro-MVP series.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-mvp-scoping.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
